summary: Russia launched a massive combined airstrike with 22 missiles and 524 Shahed‑type UAVs targeting logistics corridors in Dnipro, Dnipropetrovsk, Odesa, Chernihiv and Zaporizhzhia, achieving 507 interceptions. Ukraine accelerated anti‑drone road defenses, now protecting over 1,170 km of supply routes and constructing 8.5 km of defenses daily. UAV activity remains high across all fronts, with multiple groups moving from the Black Sea, Crimea and Belarus border toward key cities.
key_points: Russia deployed 22 missiles and 524 UAVs in a coordinated strike, 507 targets downed.
Ukraine doubled anti‑drone road‑defense construction to 8.5 km per day.
Over 1,170 km of logistical routes now have anti‑drone coverage.
UAV groups observed moving from the Black Sea toward Odesa and from Crimea toward Kharkiv and Dnipro.
Multiple Shahed attacks reported on Dnipro, Cherkasy, Odesa, and Vinnytsia regions.
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 4 Iskander‑K missiles and numerous UAVs.
Continued UAV sightings across Poltava, Zhytomyr, Sumy, Chernihiv and Kharkiv indicate persistent Russian aerial pressure.

sections.executive_summary: Russia executed a large‑scale airstrike with 22 missiles and 524 Shahed‑type UAVs, focusing on Dnipro and Dnipropetrovsk logistics hubs, while Ukraine rapidly expanded anti‑drone road defenses to over 1,170 km, constructing 8.5 km daily. The strike was largely blunted, with 507 aerial targets destroyed, but UAV activity persists across all fronts. Ongoing UAV movements from the Black Sea, Crimea and the Belarus border threaten key urban and supply nodes.

points: The Ministry of Defense ordered a rapid scale‑up of anti‑drone road defenses, increasing construction speed from 4 km to 8.5 km per day and completing 430 km between February and April, now protecting over 1,170 km of logistical routes (Source: @DeepStateUA/23516).

Ukrainian air defense units shot down 4 Iskander‑K cruise missiles launched from Crimea and intercepted 503 hostile UAVs of various types, including Shahed, Gerbera and Parody models (Source: @kpszsu/62173).

Ukrainian forces reported a missile strike on a residential block in Dnipro that injured 18 civilians, including two children, highlighting the human cost of the aerial campaign (Source: @UkraineNow/81285).

Multiple UAV groups were observed moving from the Black Sea toward Odesa and from Crimea toward Kharkiv, indicating continued Russian aerial pressure on southern and eastern supply lines (Source: @kpszsu/62166).
Ukrainian radar and air‑defence units reported 7 Shahed attacks on Vinnytsia and 4 on Cherkasy, with interceptions preventing further damage (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66800).

Russia launched 22 missiles, including 14 Iskander‑M/S‑400 ballistic missiles from Rostov and Crimea, and 8 Iskander‑K cruise missiles from Crimea, targeting Dnipro, Dnipropetrovsk, Odesa, Chernihiv and Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/62173).

A total of 524 hostile UAVs were deployed, originating from launch sites in Bryansk, Kursk, Orel, Millerovo, Primorsko-Akhtarsk, Donetsk (occupied), and Crimea, with flight paths toward central and eastern Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/62173).
Russian forces conducted a coordinated UAV attack on the Dnipro‑Dniprovska logistics corridor, attempting to disrupt supply lines and medical evacuations (Source: @DeepStateUA/23516).

Additional UAV groups were reported moving from the Black Sea toward Odesa and from the north of Chernihiv along the Belarus border toward Kyiv, indicating a multi‑axis pressure strategy (Source: @kpszsu/62170).
Russian missile and UAV strikes resulted in confirmed hits on 34 locations, with debris from downed UAVs falling in 11 separate areas (Source: @UkraineNow/81287).
UAV groups from Crimea moved toward Bogoduhiv in Kharkiv region, threatening eastern supply routes (Source: @kpszsu/62145).
Multiple Shahed attacks were reported in Dnipropetrovsk oblast, including near Sofiyivka and Shyrokyi, with Ukrainian air defenses engaging the threats (Source: @eRadarrua/92462).
UAV activity from the north of Sumy heading toward Zinkiv/Gadyach in Poltava indicates pressure on the northeastern axis (Source: @eRadarrua/92489).
A new UAV group from the Black Sea moved toward Odesa, adding to southern front pressure (Source: @kpszsu/62166).
Ukrainian anti‑drone road defenses were completed on three sections in Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv directions, enhancing eastern logistics security (Source: @DeepStateUA/23516).
UAV groups were observed moving along the Belarus border into Kyiv region, originating from Chernihiv north, indicating a northern infiltration route (Source: @kpszsu/62170).
Multiple Shahed attacks were reported in Vinnytsia and Zhytomyr, with 7 UAVs heading toward Zhytomyr from Kyiv direction (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66800).
UAV sightings in Chernihiv included groups heading toward Chernihiv, Horodnia and Slavutych, suggesting sustained pressure on the northern corridor (Source: @kpszsu/62166).
A reactive Shahed was detected over Chernihiv from the north, prompting immediate air‑defence response (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66821).
UAV groups from the north of Chernihiv moved along the Belarus border toward Kyiv, potentially targeting command and control nodes (Source: @kpszsu/62171).
New UAV groups from the Black Sea headed toward Odesa, increasing the threat to southern ports and logistics (Source: @kpszsu/62166).
Shahed attacks were reported in Odesa region, with 4 UAVs intercepted and additional groups moving from the Black Sea (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66800).
UAVs from the south of Dnipropetrovsk moved toward Dnipro, indicating pressure on the central‑southern axis (Source: @kpszsu/62171).
Ukrainian anti‑drone road defenses were expanded in five oblasts, including restoration of over 60 km of roads in southern directions (Source: @DeepStateUA/23516).
A Shahed strike on a Chinese merchant vessel in Ukrainian waters near Odesa was reported, marking a novel target selection (Source: @UkraineNow/81288).
Ukrainian air defence shot down 4 Iskander‑K cruise missiles and 503 UAVs, achieving a 96% interception rate during the May 18‑19 combined strike (Source: @kpszsu/62173).
Radar reports logged 22 missile launches and 524 UAVs, with 18 missiles and 16 UAVs confirmed to have hit 34 locations before interception (Source: @UkraineNow/81287).
Ukrainian forces reported a cruise missile crossing the Kryvyi Rih district toward Solone, highlighting missile trajectory diversity (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66808).
Multiple Shahed clusters were observed over Dnipro, Cherkasy, and Vinnytsia, prompting mobile fire groups and REB units to engage (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66800).
Ukrainian air‑defence units continue to adapt with mobile anti‑drone systems protecting over 1,170 km of supply routes (Source: @DeepStateUA/23516).

key_developments: Russia launched 22 missiles, including 14 Iskander‑M/S‑400 ballistic missiles and 8 Iskander‑K cruise missiles, targeting Dnipro, Dnipropetrovsk, Odesa, Chernihiv and Zaporizhzhia, with 507 aerial targets intercepted (Source: @kpszsu/62173).
Ukraine doubled anti‑drone road‑defense construction speed to 8.5 km per day, now protecting over 1,170 km of logistical routes across five oblasts (Source: @DeepStateUA/23516).
Ukrainian air defence shot down 4 Iskander‑K missiles and 503 hostile UAVs, achieving a 96% kill rate during the May 18‑19 combined strike (Source: @kpszsu/62173).
New UAV groups from the Black Sea moved toward Odesa, increasing the threat to southern ports and supply lines (Source: @kpszsu/62166).
Shahed attacks were reported on Dnipro (7 UAVs), Cherkasy (4 UAVs), and Vinnytsia (7 UAVs), with interceptions preventing major damage (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66800).
UAV groups were observed moving along the Belarus border into Kyiv region, indicating a northern infiltration corridor (Source: @kpszsu/62170).
A Russian missile strike on a residential block in Dnipro injured 18 civilians, including two children, underscoring the humanitarian impact of the air campaign (Source: @UkraineNow/81285).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces continue to employ large‑scale combined missile and UAV attacks to disrupt Ukrainian logistics, focusing on Dnipro and southern corridors. Ukraine's rapid expansion of anti‑drone road defenses and high interception rates mitigate but do not eliminate the threat. Persistent UAV movements from the Black Sea, Crimea and the Belarus border keep pressure on multiple fronts, requiring sustained air‑defence and mobile response capabilities.
sections.regional_response: Western partners are increasing logistical support, as evidenced by accelerated anti‑drone infrastructure deployment in Ukraine. NATO allies are monitoring the heightened UAV activity and may consider additional air‑defence aid. Regional states, notably Poland, are enhancing support facilities such as the new Abrams engine service center, bolstering Ukrainian armored capabilities.
sections.intelligence_notes: UAV launch sites remain dispersed across occupied territories, with notable activity from Crimea, the Black Sea coast, and Belarus border zones. The high volume of Shahed drones suggests continued Russian reliance on low‑cost loitering munitions. Interception data indicates Ukrainian air‑defence systems are effective but stretched, highlighting the need for further radar and missile resupply.
